title: Atlassian adds agent coordination, observability and cost controls to Jira
friendlyTitle: Jira adds tools to manage AI coding agents
summary: Atlassian has announced Jira features for planning agent-ready work, assigning tasks to Claude Code, Cursor and GitHub Copilot, tracking agent sessions, and automating routine engineering loops. The release also adds cost-management features intended to map AI spend and token use to teams, projects and pull requests.
friendlySummary: Atlassian is adding Jira tools to give coding tasks to AI agents, see their progress and track AI costs. People can still review the work before it is shipped.
whyItMatters: The bottleneck in AI-assisted development is increasingly coordination, context and review rather than code generation alone. Integrating agents into the system of record can make their work easier to inspect, govern and improve.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This may help software teams use AI agents without losing track of what they did or how much they cost.
